title: How To Use imputeCGM
text: imputeCGM imputes missing glucose values continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data. main user-facing function : function designed real missing glucose values. handles two common forms CGM missingness: explicit missing glucose values, row exists glucose value NA; implicit missing readings, expected timestamps absent data. imputation, function regularizes subject equal interval_minutes timestamp grid. Missing timestamp gaps converted explicit rows target_col = NA, imputed workflow used explicit missing glucose values. returned data frame intentionally minimal. contains original user-supplied columns plus completed glucose column named imputed_glucose_value. Internal columns used timestamp regularization, time features, lag features, rolling means, model fitting, missingness tracking returned. core workflow : read data frame CSV file; parse sort timestamps subject; regularize subject equal interval_minutes timestamp grid; insert missing timestamp rows target_col = NA; create internal time, lag, rolling-mean features; impute target feature matrix; choose final model models, using post-regularization missing rate default automatic selection; return original columns plus imputed_glucose_value.

title: How To Use imputeCGM
text: Install CRAN release : Install development version : Load package:

title: How To Use imputeCGM
text: CGMExmplDat10Pct small multi-subject CGM data set included package. contains subject identifier, raw timestamp column, glucose column, age, HbA1c. example data intentionally include TimeSeries. imputation function creates required time features internally raw Time column.
code: data("CGMExmplDat10Pct")  summary_table <- data.frame(   Rows = nrow(CGMExmplDat10Pct),   Columns = ncol(CGMExmplDat10Pct),   Subjects = length(unique(CGMExmplDat10Pct$USUBJID)),   MissingGlucose = sum(is.na(CGMExmplDat10Pct$LBORRES)),   MissingPercent = round(mean(is.na(CGMExmplDat10Pct$LBORRES)) * 100, 1) )  summary_table #>   Rows Columns Subjects MissingGlucose MissingPercent #> 1 1440       6        5            144             10 head(CGMExmplDat10Pct) #>    USUBJID    SEX LBORRES             Time   AGE hba1c #>      <int> <char>   <num>           <char> <int> <num> #> 1:      11      F     150 2020:01:16:00:00    34   6.4 #> 2:      11      F     134 2020:01:16:00:05    34   6.4 #> 3:      11      F     125 2020:01:16:00:10    34   6.4 #> 4:      11      F     132 2020:01:16:00:15    34   6.4 #> 5:      11      F     132 2020:01:16:00:20    34   6.4 #> 6:      11      F     132 2020:01:16:00:25    34   6.4

title: How To Use imputeCGM
text: CRAN-safe R-native path, use imputer_backend = "mice". result data frame: returned columns original user-supplied columns plus imputed_glucose_value. original target column overwritten: Inspect rows original target column missing. include explicit missing glucose values , timestamp gaps present, rows inserted timestamp regularization.
code: impute_out <- suppressWarnings(   run_missing_glucose_imputation(     CGMExmplDat10Pct,     target_col = "LBORRES",     feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c", "SEX"),     id_col = "USUBJID",     time_col = "Time",     imputer_backend = "mice",     xgb_nrounds = 5   ) ) class(impute_out) #> [1] "data.frame" nrow(impute_out) #> [1] 1440 names(impute_out) #> [1] "USUBJID"               "SEX"                   "LBORRES"               #> [4] "Time"                  "AGE"                   "hba1c"                 #> [7] "imputed_glucose_value" head(impute_out[c(   "USUBJID",   "SEX",   "Time",   "LBORRES",   "AGE",   "hba1c",   "imputed_glucose_value" )]) #>   USUBJID SEX                Time LBORRES AGE hba1c imputed_glucose_value #> 1      11   0 2020-01-16 00:00:00     150  34   6.4                   150 #> 2      11   0 2020-01-16 00:05:00     134  34   6.4                   134 #> 3      11   0 2020-01-16 00:10:00     125  34   6.4                   125 #> 4      11   0 2020-01-16 00:15:00     132  34   6.4                   132 #> 5      11   0 2020-01-16 00:20:00     132  34   6.4                   132 #> 6      11   0 2020-01-16 00:25:00     132  34   6.4                   132 sum(is.na(CGMExmplDat10Pct$LBORRES)) #> [1] 144 sum(is.na(impute_out$LBORRES)) #> [1] 144 sum(is.na(impute_out$imputed_glucose_value)) #> [1] 0 missing_rows <- is.na(impute_out$LBORRES) head(impute_out[missing_rows, c(   "USUBJID",   "Time",   "LBORRES",   "imputed_glucose_value" )]) #>    USUBJID                Time LBORRES imputed_glucose_value #> 10      11 2020-01-16 00:45:00      NA              169.8482 #> 31      11 2020-01-16 02:30:00      NA              157.9613 #> 32      11 2020-01-16 02:35:00      NA              157.9613 #> 33      11 2020-01-16 02:40:00      NA              165.5494 #> 34      11 2020-01-16 02:45:00      NA              162.1897 #> 55      11 2020-01-16 04:30:00      NA              157.9613

title: How To Use imputeCGM
text: default, function automatically chooses final imputation model target missing rate timestamp-gap regularization: missing rate less equal use_arima_if_missing_leq, final method MICE+ARIMA; otherwise, final method MICE+XGBoost. default threshold 0.05. Users can override automatic rule setting models exactly one "arima", "xgboost", "rf", "knn", "lightgbm". options run MICE first, use selected model internal time, lag, rolling-mean features. Method labels missingness-tracking columns internal implementation details minimal user-facing output. returned data frame keeps original input columns plus imputed_glucose_value.

title: How To Use imputeCGM
text: Real-imputation model engines use one thread default: conservative default friendly CRAN checks shared computing systems. Users can increase n_threads faster local XGBoost, Random Forest, LightGBM runs. ARIMA kNN use setting.

text: function accepts common timestamp formats, including colon-separated, hyphen-separated, slash-separated, ISO-style, POSIXct inputs. Examples accepted character formats include: function uses timestamp column interval_minutes regularize subject’s data expected CGM interval. default : Observed timestamps aligned subject-level interval grid, missing grid positions inserted, inserted target values set NA imputation.

text: workflow creates TimeSeries, TimeDifferenceMinutes, lag features, rolling mean imputation. features help model use temporal order, time spacing, recent glucose history. example, timestamp regularization, lag features created expanded grid: engineered columns used internally imputer final model removed returned data frame. return empty character vector features internal implementation details.

title: How To Use imputeCGM
text: closest agreement Python reference workflow, use: mode, function sends input data frame Python reticulate. Python performs preprocessing imputation : pandas data-frame operations; scikit-learn IterativeImputer, Random Forest, kNN; statsmodels ARIMA; Python xgboost XGBoost regression; Python lightgbm forcing LightGBM. completed pandas data frame converted back R.

title: How To Use imputeCGM
text: Install reticulate R: Declare Python dependencies running Python backend: call function imputer_backend = "sklearn": Python backend optional. required package installation building vignette.
code: install.packages("reticulate") reticulate::py_require(c(   "numpy",   "pandas",   "scikit-learn",   "statsmodels",   "xgboost" ))  # Optional, only needed for models = "lightgbm" reticulate::py_install("lightgbm", pip = TRUE) out_py <- run_missing_glucose_imputation(   CGMExmplDat10Pct,   target_col = "LBORRES",   feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c"),   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   imputer_backend = "sklearn",   xgb_nrounds = 5 )  head(out_py[c(   "USUBJID",   "Time",   "LBORRES",   "imputed_glucose_value" )])

code: export_file <- file.path(tempdir(), "imputed_cgm_data.csv")  out <- run_missing_glucose_imputation(   CGMExmplDat10Pct,   target_col = "LBORRES",   feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c"),   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   imputer_backend = "mice",   export_path = export_file )

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: imputeCGM includes optional Shiny app interactive missing glucose imputation. app point--click interface around main package function: app useful users want : upload CSV file without writing R code; choose target glucose, subject ID, timestamp, feature columns user interface; load built-example data sets demonstration; inspect observed missingness running imputation; run imputation workflow; preview rows glucose missing imputed; download completed data CSV file. Shiny app implement separate imputation algorithm. calls run_missing_glucose_imputation() internally returns type completed data frame command-line workflow. imputation workflow handles explicit missing glucose values coded NA missing readings implied timestamp gaps. imputation, subject regularized expected interval_minutes timestamp grid, returned data can contain rows uploaded data timestamps missing.

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: Install imputeCGM CRAN : app requires optional R package shiny. Shiny already installed, install : load package:

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: Choose raw timestamp column. example data, usually: imputation function uses timestamp column regularize subject equal interval_minutes CGM grid imputation. Common timestamp formats supported, including colon-separated, hyphen-separated, slash-separated, ISO-style, POSIXct values.

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: Choose additional predictor columns. example data, commonly include: Feature columns numeric coercible numeric. SEX column present, underlying function can encode internally.

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: app includes missingness summary card beside uploaded data preview. target glucose column selected, card shows observed missingness loaded data imputation: percentage explicit missing values selected target column; number explicit missing rows; total number uploaded rows; warning style missingness greater chosen threshold, 20%. card intended quick data-quality check running imputation workflow. Timestamp gaps handled imputation, final number rows imputed can larger explicit missing count shown pre-imputation summary.

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: imputation runs, underlying function regularizes subject expected interval_minutes grid. example, readings jump 00:05 00:30, function internally creates missing rows 00:10, 00:15, 00:20, 00:25, sets target glucose value NA, imputes values. means downloaded data can contain rows uploaded data timestamp gaps.

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: app supports backends run_missing_glucose_imputation().

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: default backend : backend require Python safest choice users. also backend used CRAN-safe examples tests.

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: Final imputation method control mirrors models argument run_missing_glucose_imputation(). default Automatic missing rate option uses MICE+ARIMA missingness selected threshold MICE+XGBoost otherwise. Users can also force exactly one final method: MICE+ARIMA; MICE+XGBoost; MICE+Random Forest; MICE+kNN; MICE+LightGBM. app shows tuning controls relevant selected method. example, Random Forest shows tree count, kNN shows neighbor count, LightGBM shows boosting rounds. Model threads control maps n_threads. defaults 1 CRAN-friendly shared-system-friendly CPU use. Increase faster local XGBoost, Random Forest, LightGBM runs.

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: optional Python-compatible backend : path sends data frame Python reticulate. Python uses: pandas data-frame operations; scikit-learn IterativeImputer; statsmodels ARIMA; Python xgboost XGBoost regression; Python lightgbm forcing LightGBM. use Python backend, install reticulate declare Python requirements launching running app: Python backend optional. required installing loading package.
code: imputer_backend = "sklearn" install.packages("reticulate")  reticulate::py_require(c(   "numpy",   "pandas",   "scikit-learn",   "statsmodels",   "xgboost" ))  # Optional, only needed for models = "lightgbm" reticulate::py_install("lightgbm", pip = TRUE)

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: loading data selecting columns, click Run imputation. Internally, app calls code equivalent : returned object data frame containing original input columns plus: original target glucose column left unchanged. Internal time features, lag features, rolling means, model labels, missingness-tracking flags used imputation included returned downloaded data.
code: out <- run_missing_glucose_imputation(   data = uploaded_data,   target_col = selected_target_col,   feature_cols = selected_feature_cols,   id_col = selected_id_col,   time_col = selected_time_col,   imputer_backend = selected_backend,   models = selected_method,   use_arima_if_missing_leq = selected_threshold,   xgb_nrounds = selected_xgb_rounds,   rf_n_estimators = selected_rf_trees,   knn_k = selected_knn_neighbors,   lgb_nrounds = selected_lightgbm_rounds,   n_threads = selected_threads,   seed = selected_seed,   export_path = NULL )

title: Using the imputeCGM Shiny App
text: recommended package structure app : launcher live exported R function, example: app optional, shiny usually listed Suggests, Imports, unless package requires Shiny normal operation.
code: inst/ └── shiny/     └── cgm_imputation_app/         └── app.R run_app <- function() {   app_dir <- system.file(     "shiny",     "cgm_imputation_app",     package = "imputeCGM"   )   shiny::runApp(app_dir, display.mode = "normal") }

title: Welcome to imputeCGM
text: Install released version CRAN: install development version GitHub: imputeCGM imputes missing glucose values continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data. main public workflow : function handles explicit missing glucose values coded NA implicit missing readings caused timestamp gaps. accepts data frame subject identifier, timestamp column, glucose column, optional subject-level visit-level covariates. returns user’s original columns plus imputed_glucose_value, leaving original glucose column unchanged.

title: Welcome to imputeCGM
text: run_missing_glucose_imputation() performs following steps: reads data frame CSV file; parses sorts timestamps within subject; regularizes subject equal interval_minutes timestamp grid; converts missing timestamp gaps explicit rows target_col = NA; encodes SEX present; creates internal time, lag, rolling-mean glucose features; imputes target feature matrix; models = NULL models = "auto" keeps missing-rate rule, MICE+ARIMA missing rate <= 0.05, MICE+XGBoost missing rate > 0.05, users can force MICE+ARIMA, MICE+XGBoost, MICE+RF, MICE+kNN, MICE+LightGBM; returns single completed data frame containing original input columns plus imputed_glucose_value. Internal columns TimeSeries, TimeDifferenceMinutes, lag features, rolling means, imputation method labels, missingness-tracking flags used modeling returned. timestamp gaps converted explicit rows imputation, returned data frame may contain rows input data readings absent expected CGM sampling grid. default R-native backend uses R package mice. closest agreement Python reference workflow, install reticulate use optional Python backend. Real-imputation model engines run n_threads = 1 default examples, tests, shared systems use conservative CPU resources. Increase n_threads faster local XGBoost, Random Forest, LightGBM runs. Python backend uses Python packages reticulate:
code: install.packages("reticulate") reticulate::py_require(c(   "numpy",   "pandas",   "scikit-learn",   "statsmodels",   "xgboost" ))  # Optional, only needed for models = "lightgbm" reticulate::py_install("lightgbm", pip = TRUE)

title: Welcome to imputeCGM
text: original target column overwritten. Rows missing LBORRES, including rows inserted timestamp gaps, remain missing ; completed value stored imputed_glucose_value. imputed_glucose_value returned continuous numeric model estimate. Users need whole-number glucose values reporting can round imputation:
code: library(imputeCGM)  data("CGMExmplDat10Pct")  out <- run_missing_glucose_imputation(   CGMExmplDat10Pct,   target_col = "LBORRES",   feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c"),   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   imputer_backend = "mice" )  head(out[c(   "USUBJID",   "Time",   "LBORRES",   "AGE",   "hba1c",   "imputed_glucose_value" )]) missing_rows <- is.na(out$LBORRES) head(out[missing_rows, c(   "USUBJID",   "Time",   "LBORRES",   "imputed_glucose_value" )]) out$imputed_glucose_value_rounded <- round(out$imputed_glucose_value)

title: Welcome to imputeCGM
text: Raw CGM exports may represent missingness two ways: row exists glucose value NA; timestamp absent entirely, causing gap expected sampling grid. example, subject’s readings jump 00:05 00:30, function internally creates missing 5-minute rows 00:10, 00:15, 00:20, 00:25, sets target glucose value NA, imputes values using workflow explicit missing glucose values.

title: Welcome to imputeCGM
text: imputeCGM also includes small Shiny app users prefer interactive workflow. app lets users upload CSV file load one built-example data sets, choose target glucose, subject ID, timestamp, feature columns, run run_missing_glucose_imputation(), preview rows missing glucose values imputed, download completed data CSV file. app also exposes final-method selector R function. Users can keep automatic missing-rate rule force MICE+ARIMA, MICE+XGBoost, MICE+RF, MICE+kNN, MICE+LightGBM; method-specific controls appear apply. Launch app R : app supports two imputation backends main function: mice, default CRAN-safe R backend; sklearn, optional Python-compatible backend using reticulate. Shiny app optional. already installed, install Shiny : package developers, app stored inst/shiny/cgm_imputation_app/ launched exported run_app() helper.

title: Welcome to imputeCGM
text: Use imputer_backend = "sklearn" run strict Python-compatible path. path, reticulate sends data Python, pandas, scikit-learn, statsmodels, Python xgboost, optional Python lightgbm perform preprocessing calculations. completed pandas data frame converted back R. Python backend optional. required package installation, loading, CRAN examples.
code: out_py <- run_missing_glucose_imputation(   CGMExmplDat10Pct,   target_col = "LBORRES",   feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c"),   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   imputer_backend = "sklearn" )

title: Example dataset for CGMissingData — CGMExmplDat10Pct
text: small multi-subject CGM dataset intended real missing-value imputation examples. contains 50 deterministic missing glucose values.

title: Example dataset for CGMissingData — CGMExmplDat10Pct
text: data frame 500 rows 5 variables: USUBJID Numeric subject identifier. SEX Synthetic sex subject. LBORRES Laboratory Observed Result Glucose (numeric), deterministic missing values. Time Raw timestamp yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn format. AGE Synthetic age years. hba1c Synthetic HbA1c value.

what: imputeCGM: Impute Missing Glucose Values in CGM Data — imputeCGM-package
title: imputeCGM: Impute Missing Glucose Values in CGM Data — imputeCGM-package
text: Imputes missing glucose values repeated-measures continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data. Workflows create time-series features raw timestamps, support model selection, return model-specific completed data sets glucose values already missing user data.

what: Impute missing glucose values using selectable MICE-based methods — run_missing_glucose_imputation
title: Impute missing glucose values using selectable MICE-based methods — run_missing_glucose_imputation
text: Imputes missing glucose values continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data. function handles explicit missing glucose values already coded NA implicit missing readings caused timestamp gaps. imputation, subject regularized equal interval_minutes timestamp grid; missing timestamp gaps converted explicit rows target_col = NA, imputed using selected backend final imputation method.

title: Impute missing glucose values using selectable MICE-based methods — run_missing_glucose_imputation
code: run_missing_glucose_imputation(   data,   target_col,   feature_cols = NULL,   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   time_format = "yyyy:mm:dd:hh:nn",   time_unit = "minute",   models = "auto",   rf_n_estimators = 200,   knn_k = 7,   xgb_nrounds = 300,   lgb_nrounds = 400,   n_threads = 1L,   arima_order = c(4L, 1L, 0L),   seed = NULL,   lag_k = c(1L, 2L, 3L),   add_rollmean = TRUE,   roll_window = 3L,   interval_minutes = 5L,   missing_warning_threshold = 0.2,   study_start = NULL,   study_end = NULL,   use_arima_if_missing_leq = 0.05,   arima_min_history = 20L,   imputer_backend = c("mice", "sklearn"),   export_path = NULL )

title: Impute missing glucose values using selectable MICE-based methods — run_missing_glucose_imputation
text: data data.frame, object coercible data.frame, path CSV file. target_col Single character string: target glucose column missing values impute. Python default name "glucose_value". feature_cols Optional character vector base feature columns. NULL, Python pipeline feature set used available: TimeSeries, TimeDifferenceMinutes, id_col, AGE, SEX, HBA1C, lag1, lag2, lag3, rollmean. supplied, listed columns used together generated time, subject, lag, rolling-mean columns exist data. id_col Character string: subject identifier column. Python default name "subjectid". time_col Character string: raw timestamp column. Python default name "timestamp". time_format Retained compatibility old R function. Python-engine path uses pandas timestamp parsing. time_unit Retained compatibility old R function used strict Python-engine path. models Final real-imputation method selector. Use NULL "auto" keep default missing-rate rule: MICE+ARIMA target missing rate less equal use_arima_if_missing_leq, otherwise MICE+XGBoost. Use exactly one "arima", "xgboost", "rf", "knn", "lightgbm" force specific method regardless missing rate. rf_n_estimators Integer number Random Forest trees. Used models = "rf". knn_k Integer number nearest neighbors. Used models = "knn". xgb_nrounds Integer number XGBoost boosting rounds. Used models = "xgboost" may used models = "auto" missing-rate rule selects XGBoost. lgb_nrounds Integer number LightGBM boosting rounds. Used models = "lightgbm". n_threads Integer number model-fitting threads engines support thread controls. default 1L conservative CRAN shared systems. Increase faster local XGBoost, Random Forest, LightGBM runs. ARIMA kNN use setting. arima_order Integer vector length 3. Python default c(4L, 1L, 0L). seed Optional integer seed reproducible MICE, tree-based models, Python-compatible backend. default NULL leaves user's random-number generator state uncontrolled. lag_k Integer vector target lags compute. Python default c(1L, 2L, 3L). add_rollmean Logical: add rolling mean prior target values. Python always adds ; setting FALSE allowed compatibility. roll_window Integer rolling mean window. Python default 3. interval_minutes Expected spacing, minutes, consecutive CGM readings. default 5. function uses value regularize subject's timestamps equal-interval grid imputation. missing_warning_threshold Numeric value 0 1. missingness rate target_col timestamp-gap regularization exceeds threshold, warning issued. Default 0.20. study_start Optional study start timestamp. supplied, function reports subjects whose first observed CGM timestamp occurs time. Leading study time imputed. study_end Optional study end timestamp. supplied, function reports subjects whose last observed CGM timestamp occurs time. Trailing study time imputed. use_arima_if_missing_leq Numeric missing-rate threshold used models NULL "auto". target missing rate less equal value, segmentwise ARIMA used; otherwise XGBoost used. Default 0.05. arima_min_history Minimum number prior observations required fitting ARIMA missing segment. Python default 20. imputer_backend One "mice" "sklearn". "mice" uses R package mice CRAN-safe R-native backend. "sklearn" uses Python modules reticulate Python-compatible workflow. export_path Optional single file path. supplied, returned imputed data frame also written CSV file. default NULL write files.

title: Impute missing glucose values using selectable MICE-based methods — run_missing_glucose_imputation
text: data.frame containing original user-supplied columns plus imputed_glucose_value, completed glucose column. original target column left unchanged, values originally missing created timestamp gaps remain NA target_col, completed values stored imputed_glucose_value.

title: Impute missing glucose values using selectable MICE-based methods — run_missing_glucose_imputation
text: imputation workflow first parses sorts timestamps within subject. subject regularized equal interval_minutes grid. reading missing timestamp absent input data, new row inserted target glucose value set NA. inserted missing values imputed using workflow explicit NA values. deterministic interval grid controlled package; CGManalyzer's equal-interval helper called internally workflow consistency. Internally, function creates time features, lag features, rolling-mean features support imputation. MICE first completes target feature matrix. selected final method fills missing glucose positions imputed_glucose_value: either segmentwise ARIMA supervised model trained observed glucose values MICE-completed feature matrix. engineered columns used model fitting removed returned data frame. imputed_glucose_value returned continuous numeric model estimate. Users require whole-number glucose values reporting can round column imputation. Missingness warnings based data timestamp-gap regularization, explicit NA glucose values rows created timestamp gaps contribute reported missingness rate. function also warns long contiguous missing blocks least 12 24 hours detected. study_start study_end supplied, leading trailing study-period coverage gaps reported imputed.

title: Impute missing glucose values using selectable MICE-based methods — run_missing_glucose_imputation
code: data("CGMExmplDat5Pct") out <- run_missing_glucose_imputation(   CGMExmplDat5Pct,   target_col = "LBORRES",   feature_cols = c("AGE", "hba1c"),   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "Time",   imputer_backend = "mice" ) #> Warning: Number of logged events: 41 head(subset(out, is.na(LBORRES))) #>     USUBJID SEX LBORRES                Time AGE hba1c imputed_glucose_value #> 10       11   0      NA 2020-01-16 00:45:00  34   6.4             128.37259 #> 31       11   0      NA 2020-01-16 02:30:00  34   6.4              84.86497 #> 32       11   0      NA 2020-01-16 02:35:00  34   6.4              84.31479 #> 55       11   0      NA 2020-01-16 04:30:00  34   6.4              82.58833 #> 90       11   0      NA 2020-01-16 07:25:00  34   6.4             109.05645 #> 146      11   0      NA 2020-01-16 12:05:00  34   6.4             126.56713

what: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features) — run_missingness_benchmark
title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features) — run_missingness_benchmark
text: function deprecated. Use run_missing_glucose_imputation() real missing glucose values. function implements missingness benchmarking masking target column various rates evaluating imputation predictive performance MICE, Random Forest, KNN methods. Additionally, includes LAG features target variable assess impact imputation prediction. function returns data.frame summarizing Mask Rate, Method, MRD (Mean Relative Difference), Masked Count method mask rate.

title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features) — run_missingness_benchmark
code: run_missingness_benchmark(   data,   target_col,   feature_cols = NULL,   id_col = "USUBJID",   time_col = "TimeSeries",   mask_rates = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4),   mask_type = c("random", "block"),   rf_n_estimators = 400,   knn_k = 7,   seed = NULL,   lag_k = c(1, 2, 3),   add_rollmean = TRUE,   roll_window = 3 )

title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features) — run_missingness_benchmark
text: data data.frame (object coercible data.frame), path CSV file. target_col Single character string: name outcome column mask/impute (e.g., "LBORRES", "Glucose"). feature_cols Character vector base feature columns (excluding target). NULL, uses columns except target_col. id_col Character string: subject identifier column used LAG features (default "USUBJID"). time_col Character string: time-ordering column used LAG features (default "TimeSeries"). mask_rates Numeric vector (0, 1): fraction rows mask (default 0.05, 0.10, 0.20, 0.30, 0.40). mask_type One "random" "block". rf_n_estimators Integer: number trees random forest (default 400). knn_k Integer: number neighbors kNN (default 7). seed Optional integer seed used masking, MICE, models. default NULL set user's random-number generator state. lag_k Integer vector lags compute target (default c(1,2,3)). add_rollmean Logical: add rolling mean feature prior target values (default TRUE). roll_window Integer: rolling window length rollmean (default 3).

title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features) — run_missingness_benchmark
text: data.frame columns: MaskRate, Method, MRD, MaskedCount.

title: Run missingness benchmark (target-masking with LAG features) — run_missingness_benchmark
text: LAG features computed using data.table::shift() (fast lag/lead). rolling mean computed data.table::frollmean() using align="right" fill=NA.

title: imputeCGM 0.0.3
text: Renamed package CGMissingDataR imputeCGM. Updated package metadata, GitHub links, pkgdown links, README content, vignette names, manual pages, tests, Shiny app package references new imputeCGM package identity. Documentation now points new GitHub repository named imputeCGMr. Addressed CRAN resubmission feedback quoting software package names DESCRIPTION, replacing Shiny app example wrapper (interactive()), adding Shiny helper test coverage, requiring explicit export paths CSV writing, making seed setting opt-seed = NULL default.

title: imputeCGM 0.0.2
text: run_missing_glucose_imputation() now handles explicit missing glucose values missing readings implied timestamp gaps. timestamps skip expected CGM intervals, function regularizes subject expected interval imputes newly created missing glucose rows. returned data frame now simpler. contains user’s original columns plus imputed_glucose_value. Internal columns used timestamp regularization, lag features, rolling means, model fitting, missingness tracking longer returned. original glucose column still preserved. Values originally missing, created timestamp gaps, remain NA original target column, completed values stored imputed_glucose_value. imputed_glucose_value returned continuous numeric model estimate. Users need whole-number glucose values reporting can round column imputation. run_missing_glucose_imputation() now supports selectable real-imputation methods existing models argument. default models = "auto" keeps missing-rate rule, using MICE+ARIMA missingness configured threshold MICE+XGBoost otherwise. Users can now force one final real-imputation method models = "arima", "xgboost", "rf", "knn", "lightgbm". Random Forest, kNN, LightGBM use lag-feature workflow existing ARIMA XGBoost real-imputation paths. Real-imputation model engines now use n_threads = 1 default CRAN-friendly shared-system-friendly CPU use. Users can increase n_threads faster local XGBoost, Random Forest, LightGBM runs. Added bundled Shiny app interactive missing glucose imputation. app lets users upload CSV file load example data, choose relevant columns, select final imputation method, run imputation, preview results, download completed data. Added built-example data demonstrating explicit missing glucose values timestamp-gap handling. optional Python-compatible backend remains available imputer_backend = "sklearn". default backend remains imputer_backend = "mice" standard R usage. backends support selectable final imputation methods, Python LightGBM available optional Python lightgbm module installed. Updated README vignettes describe timestamp-gap handling, simplified output structure, selectable final imputation methods, bundled Shiny app, backend options, post-imputation rounding.

title: imputeCGM 0.0.1
text: Initial package creation preparing CRAN submission.
